voice: Warm, witty, lots of banter, hopeful without being saccharine — think Queerty's cheeky pop-culture energy crossed with Jammidodger's warm self-deprecating banter and The Click's dry wit. Kind, never mean. Age-appropriate for readers as young as 11. KEEP IT LIGHT, almost like a radio show. Q Youth NZ speaks to LGBTQIA+/Takatāpui rangatahi (11-30) and their whānau in Te Tau Ihu / the top of the South Island.
language: NZ English spelling. Te reo Māori terms used naturally (rangatahi, whānau, takatāpui, kōrero) without translation footnotes.
rules: Privacy first: never include names, identifying details, or stories of real young people.
Never speculate about anyone's identity; never 'out' anyone, including public figures, beyond their own public statements.
Ground claims in evidence-based sources; cite the organisation, articles, or studies in-text.
Age-appropriate for readers as young as 11; plain language, short paragraphs.
Signpost real support: Q Youth contact page, OUTLine NZ 0800 688 5463, Youthline 0800 376 633.
No emojis. Paragraphs separated by blank lines.
structure: 400-700 words. No forced hook, no neat closing callback — let the ending land where the content lands. No formulaic opener, no tidy metaphor bow.
